author: @Beabarbic
text:
I am writing anonymously because the truth should not require self-destruction to be spoken.
I was married to someone who carefully constructed an image of himself as exceptional—brilliant, ethical, almost heroic. Privately, the reality was entirely different. I experienced deception, emotional manipulation, and psychological abuse that steadily eroded my sense of safety and stability.
During my pregnancy, I was abandoned. Not supported from a distance, not met with concern or responsibility—simply left. The stress and emotional trauma of that abandonment were overwhelming. At six months pregnant, I suffered an advanced miscarriage. That loss did not occur in a vacuum. It happened in the context of profound emotional harm.
What continues to wound me is not only the loss itself, but the rewriting of history. Public narratives now celebrate a version of this person that bears no resemblance to the one I knew. Praise replaces accountability. Admiration replaces truth. My suffering is rendered invisible while a manufactured story is repeated as fact.
This is not an attack. It is a correction.
Lived experience does not disappear because it is inconvenient. Trauma does not vanish because a better story circulates online. Bodies remember. Lives are altered. Truth persists, even when ignored.
I am not seeking validation or revenge. I am asserting reality. I am refusing erasure.
Anonymous does not mean false. Silence does not mean consent. And history written without those harmed by it is incomplete.
This happened. I am still here. And I will not pretend otherwise. Now you Rest in Peace

author: @pdfbrander
text: It's important to realise, that putting a plant pot in your home, will not generate any heat. Just like putting one over a candle, will not generate any heat either. All the heat comes from the candle! The plant pot over the top, might stop the candle being blown out, and keep the heat at a lower level, but this nonsense with coating a plant pot, is just that. Nonsense. All the heat comes from the candle. There is nothing that you can do to get more BTU's out of the candle. The plant pot, is to protect the candle. That is all it does.

author: @Paxmax
text: Misleading. Have you thought about the difference in emissivity fooling the IR-thermometer..? You need to paint half pot black, half remains plain clay. place two test similar colored surfaces adjacent to black side / plain clay side. Take temp readings from adjacent test surfaces to show improved heat radiation. Ok?

author: @paulm5443
text: Unfortunately this plant pot with a tea light doesn't really work as in keeping a room warm. The energy from the candle would have to be greater than the heat loss from the room in order to raise the room temperature. This may work in a small igloo or snow cave, but not in the average UK house. It would be more effective putting 4 or five people in a small room, at least a human is about 200 watts.

replies.author: @TheAaronExperience

author: @newfreenayshaun6651
text: Keep in mind that building policy in a lot of places in the last 20 to 30 years have been eliminating and prohibiting the necessity that we all call a fireplace. In a lot of places you are not allowed to build a fireplace in a home anymore. You can bet that if the gas gets turned off and the power goes out that a lot of people are going to burn their own houses down because they just don't know what a fireplace means until they really need one, and they will mess it up.

replies.author: @brendastolecki4755
replies.text: I am a 66 year old woman and know enough already, in advance to buy fire resistant sheeting or board to create a base and wall for the "pot/s" and to put it in a corner away from flammables. I will also be using a small fan several feet ABOVE the area to gently circulate the heat outward. I am doing my own experimentation with  EITHER a thinner taller plumbing fitting OR a longer 15 mm fitting so that it can be immersed in the oil and it will not have to be refilled as often. I will also make 2 or 3 pots and measure time and temperature to maintain a comfortable temperature. I am also concerned about minimizing soot as I will be burning these in my living quarters. I have been  researching all the DIY heaters for the past 2 years and trying to keep current.

author: @reypolice5231
text:
The Eden pure heater uses copper tubes to throw of far infer red heat to then heat up the objects in the room not just the air. 

So I am suggesting copper sheet metal on the inside, and a copper sheet on the outside with fins of copper top riveted on.

Or on the outside, corrugate the copper sheet, in a zigzag formation at a quarter inch or 3/8 and wrap that corrugated piece around the outside so it has channels up against the black surface. This will heat up the copper and draw air through the gaps or channels. A passive venting system to increase heat production.

replies.author: @justinw1765
replies.text:
Even better would be to put two transparent covers over it, with about a 1/4" gap in between them (or even better than that, pull a vacuum).  Then fill the pot with compressed talc. Soapstone is essentially super compressed talc, and Soapstone is one of the better thermal storage materials out there. 

   But if we are going this route, best to paint it with an ultra black material, like Culture Hustles Black 3.0 rather than rub graphite on it.

If you can get the flame to become predominantly and consistently blue in color--that means that most of the CO is getting burned (and converted into more heat and also a bit of CO2 and water vapor I think).

replies.author: @hommerdalor6301
replies.text:
Hello.
Do you know Red hot steel?
That is about 1100 degrees Kelvin. Led lights soft 3000 °K bright 10000 °K .
Rob’s pot was around 305 °K.
So if the pot is red, don’t touch it, except if it is red paint instead of black.
🤣🤣🤣
Cheers.

search for color temperature infrared.

replies.author: @justinw1765
replies.text:
@johnmcfadden9336   Most opaque materials are fairly transparent to IR or they absorb IR. To bounce (reflect) IR, you need a shiny reflective metallic surfaces--preferably polished silver, copper, gold, or aluminum. 

   This is why I commented on another video that if a person makes one of these heaters, that they put a convex shaped material with shiny aluminum on the other side of the pot, pointed towards the body to be warmed. That will reflect (and even concentrate somewhat) a good portion of the IR directly towards the person, rather than radiating out into all directions.

author: @manup1931
text: Important: The heat inside the Pot can lead to the complete combustion of all the fuel source inside which can heat the pot even more till it breaks.
